name: John Bates
headline: Blocking specialist with negligible target volume; deep waiver-wire afterthought
body:
Bates is paid to block, and the receiving numbers say exactly that. Across 2025 he ran just 171 routes on a 34.2% route share, drawing only 16 targets for a 3.7% team target share and a microscopic 0.064 WOPR. He turned that into 11 grabs for 103 yards and a score, and the efficiency was actually sharp where it counted: 0.488 EPA per play, a 62.5% success rate, and 6.18 yards after the catch per reception. The problem is the funnel, not the hands. With a 0.6 yards-per-route mark and three air yards per target, there is no path to fantasy volume.

The broader picture confirms it. He sits buried on the depth chart with no expanded role on the horizon, and projections topping out near 18 catches, 174 yards, and a couple of scores leave him as a low-end TE3 going essentially undrafted. He stays a blocking-first body to leave on the wire until something opens up.
